proposals.reason: ISO/TS 24971-2 published July 2026 with ML/AI risk management guidance; immediate impact on SaMD dossiers in development and notified body assessment criteria.

card.t: ISO/TS 24971-2 Published — ML/AI Risk Management Guidance
card.x: Technical specification extends ISO/TS 24971 to cover training data, model validation, drift, and explainability; manufacturers must revise technical files to reflect ML-specific risk analysis requirements.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2026-4 finalizes SSCP role/responsibility management in EUDAMED; conformity required by end Q3 2026 for all active trial sponsors.

card.t: MDCG 2026-4 — SSCP Management in EUDAMED Finalised
card.x: Clarifies access rights, data validation workflow, EUDAMED identifier assignment, and synchronisation with investigator/site registries; deadline Q3 2026.

proposals.reason: CNIL methodologies MR-001 and MR-003 published 23 May 2026 modernise health data governance framework; immediate impact on all projects hosting health data in France.

card.t: CNIL Methodologies MR-001 & MR-003 — Health Data Framework Updated
card.x: Replace legacy MR with GDPR post-2018 requirements: quarterly audit, encryption by design, dynamic consent management, MFA, network isolation, 10+ year secure archival.

proposals.reason: Swissdamed (Swiss EUDAMED equivalent) mandatory 1 July 2026; existing stock registration required before deadline with double-submission burden for EU+CH distribution.

card.x: Centralised registry for all devices marketed in Switzerland; mandatory registration of existing stock, technical data submission, and authorized representative qualification.

proposals.reason: MHRA AI Airlock Phase 2 report published 9 June 2026 establishes objective evaluation criteria for medical AI/ML; expected to influence MDCG and ISO/TS 24971-2 harmonisation.

card.t: MHRA AI Airlock Phase 2 — Medical AI Evaluation Framework Published
card.x: Consolidates pilot feedback; defines objective criteria for training data validation, robustness testing, explainability, and cybersecurity audit across use-case types.

proposals.reason: Decision (EU) 2026/1231 adds new harmonised standards for specific device categories; manufacturers must verify applicability and update technical files accordingly.

card.t: Decision (EU) 2026/1231 — New Harmonised MDR Standards Published
card.x: Adds harmonised standards for software and implantable devices; presumption of conformity applies if standards followed; verify applicability to product class/type.

proposals.reason: Transition from EC REP to EU REP symbol for authorised representatives; applies to labelling and documentation with expected 12–18 month tolerance period.

card.t: EU REP Symbol — Transition from EC REP Identifier
card.x: Authorised representative identifier updated from EC REP to EU REP on labels and packaging; coordinate with notified body and suppliers on transition timeline.

proposals.reason: Regulation (EU) 2026/977 strengthens notified body audit requirements; enhanced documentation and traceability criteria may shorten audit cycles.

card.t: Regulation (EU) 2026/977 — Enhanced Notified Body Audit Rules
card.x: Tighter audit criteria on risk documentation, component traceability, harmonised standards compliance, and change management; anticipate accelerated audit schedules.

proposals.reason: MDCG guides 2026-1/2/3 release EMDN v2026 codes for software, AI/ML, and e-health devices; deadline for code mapping and EUDAMED/vigilance updates likely Q4 2026.

card.t: MDCG Guides 2026-1/2/3 — EMDN v2026 Nomenclature Codification
card.x: New/revised EMDN codes for medical software, AI/ML, e-health; audit existing product codes, update EUDAMED and vigilance registries before Q4 2026 harmonisation deadline.

proposals.reason: MDR Article 52.4 and 61.6 exemptions for implants take effect 1 July 2026; manufacturers may qualify for reduced clinical trial requirements if criteria met.

card.t: MDR Articles 52.4 & 61.6 — Revised Implant Clinical Trial Exemptions
card.x: Refined exemption conditions for implants with established biocompatibility and sufficient historical data; verify eligibility with notified body to potentially streamline dossier.
